cards.back: Creating multiple functions with the same name that have different parameter types

cards.front: What requirement allows two functions with the same name to coexist in the same scope?
cards.back: The functions must have different parameter types (or be otherwise differentiated by the compiler).

cards.text: Each function sharing a name in the same scope is called an {{c1::overloaded function}} or an {{c2::overload}} for short.

cards.question: What does this program output?
cards.explanation: The call add(1, 2) resolves to add(int, int) returning 3. The call add(1.2, 3.4) resolves to add(double, double) returning 4.6.

cards.back: The process by which the compiler matches a function call to the appropriate overloaded function based on the arguments

cards.text: To create an overloaded add() function for doubles: {{c1::double add(double x, double y) { return x + y; }}}

cards.front: What two conditions must be true for a program using overloaded functions to compile?
cards.back:
• Each overloaded function must be differentiated from the others
  • Each call to an overloaded function must resolve to an overloaded function

cards.question: Which add() function is called for each statement?
cards.answer:
add(1, 2) calls add(int, int)
add(1.2, 3.4) calls add(double, double)
cards.explanation: The compiler performs overload resolution by matching the argument types to the parameter types.

cards.text: Functions can be overloaded so long as {{c1::each overloaded function can be differentiated by the compiler}}.

cards.concept: Why use function overloading instead of differently named functions
cards.explanation: Function overloading reduces program complexity by reducing the number of function names to remember. Instead of addInteger(), addDouble(), addFloat(), you just use add() for all types.
examples: // Without overloading - must remember multiple names
// With overloading - one name to remember

cards.front: What happens if an overloaded function cannot be differentiated from another overloaded function?

cards.text: Best practice: {{c1::Use function overloading to make your program simpler}}.
